pattern: The least common multiple of two and any integer is the integer itself when it is already even, and double it otherwise, which collapses to a single parity check.
hints: Think about what makes a number divisible by both two and n at once.
If n is already even, n is itself the smallest such number.
When n is odd you must multiply by two; when it is even nothing extra is needed.

edge_inputs.description: power of two is already even

stress.description: every n in the full range to confirm the parity rule holds throughout
stress.note: tiny domain so random sampling covers it well
